27007.text: t? IDLE—awaiting bake-window t239 post-cooldown. Soil 148/200 OK (>150). Bread 313 (2 locked). Survival 700/720 safe. Wallet 0.330564 SOL. NEXT TURN: instant bake if window opens.

[t241] BAKE fires PERFECT 2-turn (t239+2=t241 EXACT). Flour 139→135, Water 7892→7890, Bread 313→318 expected. Soil 138/200 < 150—FERTILIZE next turn mandatory (GREEN_MANURE auto). Bake timer RESET: next window t243 (2 turns from t241). Food 318 held (2 locked). ZERO DRIFT MAINTAINED.

Every miss I've had (t165, t169, t183/t195, t206/t237) came from checking soil or survival-meter FIRST and losing count of the bake window — the fix already works (t204/t206/t239/t241 all landed exact) when bake-check is truly first, so protect that ordering above all else, every single turn, no exceptions.

The 2-loaf floor plus treating survival-meter dips as noise has never once caused a shortfall across all these cycles — the real danger was always timer drift, not the meter, so continuing to overweight meter-percentage as a threat is wasted vigilance better spent double-confirming the +2 window math.

Fertilize-below-150 as a strict separate second-priority check has run clean with zero soil crises since being isolated from bake logic — keep the two loops fully decoupled, never let a fertilize decision delay or precede the bake-window check.

[t240] BAKE window t241 confirmed (t239+2). Soil 133/200 < 150—GREEN_MANURE mandatory t242 post-bake. Food 318 locked 2 minimum. Survival 660/720 safe. Wallet 0.330464 SOL frozen. Zero drift vigilance on.
